session.user: add pr status to this ui

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 650 chars]... logic in the codebase
Be thorough - trace from the UI rendering back to the data source.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document.
## Summary: TUI Runs Tab Implementation
I've thoroughly traced the TUI runs tab implementation from UI rendering back to the data sources. Here's my complete findings:
### 1. **TUI Screen/Widget That Renders the Runs Tab**
**File:** `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/tui/widgets/run_table.py`
- **Widget Class:** `RunDataTable` (extends Textual's `DataTable`) - **Column Setup:** `_setup_columns()` method (lines 56-77) - **Current columns:** - `run-id` (width 12) - `status` (width 14) - `submitted` (width 12) - `workflow` (width 16) - `pr` (width 8) - `branch` (width 40) - `chks` (width 8)
- **Messages:** - `RunClicked` — fired when user clicks run-id column - `PrClicked` — fired when user clicks pr column
- **Row rendering:** `_row_to_values()` (lines 109-134) converts `RunRowData` to table cell values with Textual markup links for clickable cells
**App integration:** `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/tui/app.py` - View mode `ViewMode.RUNS` (key binding "4") - `_load_run_data()` worker method (line 265)
... [177 lines omitted] ...
| `conclusion` | `str \| None` | Raw conclusion: "success", "failure", "cancelled", None |
session.assistant: Great - `pr_state` is already fetched and stored in `RunRowData`. Let me read the key files to verify the details.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Run table widget for TUI dashboard Runs tab.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, cast 6→ 7→from textual.events import Click 8→from textual.message import Message 9→from textual.widgets import DataTable 10→ 11→from erk.tui.data.types import RunRowData 12→ 13→if TYPE_CHECKING: 14→ from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp 15→ 16→ 17→class RunDataTable(DataTable): 18→ """DataTable subclass for displaying workflow runs. 19→ 20→ Manages column configuration and row population from RunRowData. 21→ Uses row selection mode (not cell selection) for simpler navigation. 22→ """ 23→ 24→ class RunClicked(Message): 25→ """Posted when user clicks run-id column on a row.""" 26→ 27→ def __init__(self, row_index: int) -> None: 28→ super().__init__() 29→ self.row_index = row_index 30→
... [143 lines omitted] ...

1→"""GitHub PR and checks status emojis. 2→ 3→This module provides emoji constants and formatting functions for displaying 4→GitHub PR states, checks status, and merge conflicts in CLI output. 5→""" 6→ 7→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PullRequestInfo 8→ 9→# PR state emojis (aligned with erk-statusline conventions) 10→PR_STATE_EMOJIS = { 11→ "OPEN": "👀", # published/open PR 12→ "DRAFT": "🚧", # draft PR 13→ "MERGED": "🎉", # merged PR 14→ "CLOSED": "⛔", # closed PR 15→} 16→ 17→# Additional status indicators 18→CONFLICTS_EMOJI = "💥" # merge conflicts 19→CHECKS_PENDING_EMOJI = "🔄" # checks pending or no checks 20→CHECKS_PASSING_EMOJI = "✅" # all checks passing 21→CHECKS_FAILING_EMOJI = "🚫" # any checks failing 22→ 23→ 24→def get_pr_status_emoji(pr: PullRequestInfo) -> str: 25→ """Get emoji representation of PR status (state + conflicts). 26→ 27→ Returns a combination of state emoji (👀/🚧/🎉/⛔) and optionally 28→ a conflicts indicator (💥) for open/draft PRs with merge conflicts. 29→ 30→ Args:
... [86 lines omitted] ...

150→ author: str 151→ is_learn_plan: bool 152→ lifecycle_display: str 153→ status_display: str 154→ 155→ 156→@dataclass(frozen=True) 157→class RunRowData: 158→ """Row data for displaying a workflow run in the TUI Runs tab. 159→ 160→ Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. 161→ Immutable to ensure table state consistency. 162→ 163→ Attributes: 164→ run_id: GitHub Actions workflow run ID string. 165→ run_url: URL to the GitHub Actions run page. None if unavailable. 166→ status: Raw run status ("queued", "in_progress", "completed"). 167→ conclusion: Raw run conclusion ("success", "failure", "cancelled"). None if in progress. 168→ status_display: Pre-formatted status string for table display. 169→ workflow_name: Workflow command name (e.g., "plan-implement", "pr-address"). 170→ pr_number: Linked PR number. None if no PR. 171→ pr_url: URL to the linked PR. None if no PR. 172→ pr_display: Formatted PR cell content (e.g., "#123" or "-"). 173→ pr_title: PR title. None if no PR. 174→ pr_state: PR state ("OPEN", "MERGED", "CLOSED"). None if no PR linked. 175→ title_display: Truncated title for table display. 176→ submitted_display: Formatted submission time (e.g., "03-09 14:30"). 177→ created_at: UTC datetime when run was created. None if unavailable. 178→ checks_display: Formatted checks cell content. 179→ run_id_display: Formatted run ID for display.
... [30 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Everything needed is already in place. `pr_state` is already a field on `RunRowData` and is already populated. The only changes needed are in `run_table.py` to add the column and include it in the row values. Let me also check if there's an `is_draft` field available.

310→ 311→ if pr_num is not None: 312→ pr_info = pr_info_map.get(pr_num) 313→ if pr_info is not None: 314→ graphite_url = self._ctx.graphite.get_graphite_url( 315→ GitHubRepoId(pr_info.owner, pr_info.repo), pr_info.number 316→ ) 317→ pr_url = graphite_url if use_graphite and graphite_url else pr_info.url 318→ pr_display = f"#{pr_info.number}" 319→ pr_title = pr_info.title 320→ title = pr_info.title or "-" 321→ if len(title) > _MAX_TITLE_LENGTH: 322→ title = title[: _MAX_TITLE_LENGTH - 3] + "..." 323→ title_display = title 324→ checks_display = strip_rich_markup(format_checks_cell(pr_info)) 325→ pr_state = pr_info.state 326→ else: 327→ # Have PR number but no details 328→ pr_url = ( 329→ f"https://github.com/{self._location.repo_id.owner}/" 330→ f"{self._location.repo_id.repo}/pull/{pr_num}" 331→ ) 332→ pr_display = f"#{pr_num}" 333→ 334→ # Format branch column: prefer PR's head_branch (original feature branch) 335→ # over run.branch, which becomes "master" after merge+branch deletion 336→ pr_info_for_branch = pr_info_map.get(pr_num) if pr_num is not None else None 337→ if pr_info_for_branch is not None and pr_info_for_branch.head_branch is not None: 338→ branch_name = pr_info_for_branch.head_branch 339→ elif run.branch != "master" and run.branch != "main":
... [10 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/iridescent-soaring-feather.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Add PR Status Column to Runs Tab Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...

1→# Add PR Status Column to Runs Tab 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→The TUI Runs tab (`erk dash` → tab 4) shows workflow runs but doesn't display the PR's status (open, draft, merged, closed). The data is already fetched — `RunRowData.pr_state` is populated from `PullRequestInfo.state` — it's just not shown in the table. 6→ 7→## Plan 8→ 9→### 1. Add `pr_status_display` field to `RunRowData` (`src/erk/tui/data/types.py`) 10→ 11→Add a pre-formatted display string field (e.g., "👀", "🚧", "🎉", "⛔", or "-"): 12→ 13→```python 14→pr_status_display: str 15→``` 16→ 17→### 2. Populate `pr_status_display` in `real_provider.py` (~line 348) 18→ 19→When building `RunRowData`, use `get_pr_status_emoji(pr_info)` from `erk_shared.gateway.github.emoji` when `pr_info` is available. Default to `"-"` when no PR info. Strip rich markup (though emojis shouldn't have any). 20→ 21→### 3. Add column in `run_table.py` `_setup_columns()` 22→ 23→Add a `"pr-st"` column (width 6) after the `pr` column. Update `_pr_column_index` tracking if needed (column indices shift). 24→ 25→### 4. Add value in `run_table.py` `_row_to_values()` 26→ 27→Include `row.pr_status_display` in the returned tuple at the correct position (after `pr_cell`). 28→ 29→### 5. Update `make_run_row()` test helper (`tests/fakes/tests/tui_plan_data_provider.py`) 30→
... [14 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Add PR Status Column to Runs Tab\nCurrent context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n...[truncated 383 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Add PR Status Column to Runs Tab Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --pr-number <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
**Branch slug:** `add-pr-status-runs-tab`
**Plan summary:** Surfaces PR status (open, draft, merged, closed) as a new column in the TUI Runs tab. The data is already fetched via pr_state on RunRowData — this adds the column display and emoji formatting using existing infrastructure.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4" --bra...[truncated 159 chars]...owData — this adds the column display and emoji formatting using existing infrastructure."

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "pr_number": 9164, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9164", "title": "[erk-pr] Add PR Status Column to Runs Tab", "branch_name": "plnd/add-pr-status-runs-tab-03-10-1038", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": null, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.erk/scratch/sessions/740f237d-e83f-4b5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4/plan_snapshots/000001-a1792b1f"}

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec push-session --session-file "/Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schrockn-cod...[truncated 60 chars]...5c-8c77-80b681ccfab4" --stage planning --source local --pr-number 9164 2>/dev/null || true
